name: Base Deck

blacks.text: My doctor told me I have too much ______ in my blood.
The new Olympic sport is ______.
Scientists discover that ______ causes cancer.
My grandmother died happy, surrounded by ______ and ______.
Nobody tells you that being an adult means dealing with ______.
I want ______ playing as I walk down the aisle.
AI is coming for ______'s job.
My ex had a problem with ______.
I lost 20 pounds thanks to ______.
The reason the train was delayed was ______.
At the office we all pretend ______ doesn't exist.
My therapist told me to stop thinking about ______.
Tonight on the news: ______ threatens to destroy the economy.
The new government proposes replacing education with ______.
Instead of telling the truth, I blamed it on ______.
My superpower in meetings is ______.
What do you do when no one is watching? ______.
The secret to a lasting relationship is ______ and lots of ______.
The next Netflix reality show: Surviving ______.
I blocked my family on WhatsApp because of ______.
A tasting menu for tonight: ______ with a foam of ______.
The CEO announced the company will be doubling down on ______ going into 2026.
My retirement plan is ______.
The worst part of Christmas with family is ______.
A new study reveals that millennials have killed ______.

whites: The aunt who asks when you're getting married
Opening a beer with your teeth
Checking your phone at a funeral
Going to the gym in January
Turning on airplane mode to avoid replying
Filling in the spreadsheet wrong on purpose
Buying things on sale you don't need
The boss who sends emails at 11pm
Working out once a year and telling everyone
A meeting that could have been an email
Complaining about free time when you have it
Eating standing up over the sink
The ex who still follows you on Instagram
Talking to yourself in the car
Googling symptoms and diagnosing terminal cancer
Being overdrawn at the end of the month
Asking your parents for money at 35
The boss who can't use Excel
A team meeting on Friday at 6pm
Playing music loud to avoid thinking
The Twitter thread nobody asked for
Telling someone they look great when they don't
Sending a message to the wrong group
Making up plans to stay home
Your self from ten years ago
Comparing yourself to others on social media
A meditation app you open once
The coworker who always needs a favor
A subscription you forgot to cancel
Your boss's face on a Monday morning
Losing the thread on a video call
Remembering something you had to do
The colleague who puts you on speakerphone
Opening the fridge without knowing why
A discount that doesn't apply at checkout
Pretending to care about the environment
The last one to find out
Crying in the car before going into work
